message:
fix(signal,sync): poll-based signal tests and notify dead-code assertion

Two independent fixes:

- signal.rs: Rewrite signal_recv_observes_delivery and
  signal_recv_preserves_multiple_recorded_deliveries tests to use
  explicit poll-based assertions (Pin::new + poll with NoopWaker)
  instead of futures_lite::future::block_on. The block_on approach
  was fragile because it creates its own waker and executor, which
  can mask timing issues. Direct polling verifies exact Poll::Ready
  behavior in a single synchronous poll without executor overhead.

- notify.rs: Replace the None arm in Notified::poll's waker-update
  match with unreachable!(). The waker slot is only set to None by
  notify_one/notify_waiters (which also set notified=true, causing
  an early Ready return above) or by WaitSlab::remove (which clears
  waiter_index, so we can't reach this path with a valid index).
  This makes the invariant explicit and will catch any future
  violations immediately rather than silently re-registering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
